name: horizon
description: GPU-accelerated terminal board that puts all your sessions on an infinite canvas
readme_summary: Horizon is a GPU-accelerated terminal application that provides an infinite canvas for managing multiple terminal sessions. It allows users to organize, resize, and group terminals into workspaces, offering features like session persistence, smart detection of interactive elements, and integration with AI agents.
key_features: Infinite Canvas for terminal sessions
GPU-accelerated rendering
Workspaces with color-coding and layout modes
Full terminal emulation (24-bit color, mouse reporting, etc.)
AI Agent Panels (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, KiloCode)
Built-in Git status panel
Smart detection of URLs and file paths
Remote host discovery and connection
Live settings editor with YAML syntax highlighting
Session persistence and restoration
Markdown editor with split view and live preview
primary_use_case: Organizing and managing multiple terminal sessions on an infinite, GPU-accelerated canvas for enhanced developer productivity.
cover_image_prompt: A conceptual illustration of a vast, infinite digital canvas floating in space. On this canvas, numerous distinct 'islands' or 'panels' represent different terminal sessions, each glowing with a unique color. A central, stylized 'navigator' character, perhaps a futuristic architect or cartographer, is actively arranging these panels, resizing them with glowing tools, and connecting them with luminous pathways. Subtle UI elements like minimaps and data streams flow around the scene, hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is one of organized productivity and boundless digital space, with the repository name 'Horizon' subtly integrated into the canvas's horizon line. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, futuristic aesthetic and vibrant, glowing accents.

name: DyberPet
description: Desktop Cyber Pet Framework based on PySide6
readme_summary: DyberPet is a desktop cyber pet framework built with PySide6, allowing users to have interactive animated characters on their desktop. It supports extensive customization through a modding ecosystem for characters, items, and mini-pets, and integrates AI assistants for enhanced user interaction and daily task management.
key_features: Animated desktop pets with interactive features
AI assistant integration for chat and task management
Extensible MOD ecosystem for characters, items, and mini-pets
Task and quest systems
In-game shop functionality
Customizable JSON configurations for mods
primary_use_case: To provide users with an interactive and customizable virtual pet experience on their desktop, enhanced with AI capabilities.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical 3D isometric illustration of a cozy digital desktop environment. In the center, a charming, animated cyber pet (inspired by a stylized anime character) is interacting with a user's hand reaching out from a subtle UI element. Around the pet, floating icons represent different functionalities: a chat bubble with AI-generated text, a small to-do list, a treasure chest for items, and a glowing portal labeled 'MODS'. Digital particles and subtle code snippets float in the background, hinting at the underlying framework. The overall mood is friendly and engaging, with soft, warm lighting. The repository name 'DyberPet' is subtly integrated into a desktop widget. The style should be clean, vibrant, and inviting, clearly conveying a customizable, AI-enhanced desktop companion.

name: awesome-shizuku
description: Curated list of awesome Android apps making use of Shizuku
readme_summary: This repository is a curated list of Android applications that leverage Shizuku, a tool enabling normal apps to access system APIs with elevated privileges on non-rooted devices via ADB. It serves as a central resource for discovering and exploring the capabilities of Shizuku-enabled apps across various categories.
key_features: Curated list of Shizuku-enabled Android apps
Categorized app listings (AI agents, Automation, Customization, etc.)
Includes both open-source and closed-source applications
Provides links to app sources and details
Information on Shizuku development libraries
Daily changelogs for list updates
primary_use_case: Discovering and cataloging Android applications that utilize the Shizuku framework for enhanced functionality on non-rooted devices.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical Android robot character, normally confined to a small, standard operating area, is shown breaking free and expanding its capabilities. It's using a magical ADB wand to access and manipulate various system-level controls and features that are visually represented as glowing, intricate gears and levers floating around it. The robot is depicted with a look of empowered curiosity, interacting with these advanced controls. Subtle UI elements showing Shizuku's interface and data streams flow around the scene, connecting the robot to the expanded functionalities. The overall style is a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic, emphasizing the 'rootless' freedom granted by Shizuku.

name: start-os
description: A graphical server OS optimized for self-hosting
readme_summary: StartOS is a Rust-based Linux distribution designed for self-hosting personal servers. It simplifies the management of various services, including Bitcoin nodes, communication platforms, and cloud storage, by running them in isolated LXC containers packaged as S9PKs.
key_features: Discovery, installation, network configuration, data backup, dependency management, and health monitoring of self-hosted services
Runs services in isolated LXC containers
S9PK format for service packaging (signed, merkle-archived, partial downloads, cryptographic verification)
Custom diff-based database (Patch-DB) for reactive state synchronization
Rust backend (Tokio/Axum), Angular frontend, Node.js container runtime
primary_use_case: Enabling users to easily self-host a variety of services on a personal server, gaining full ownership of their data and infrastructure.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, bustling digital marketplace depicted as a series of interconnected stalls within a futuristic, yet cozy, personal server room. Each stall represents a different self-hosted service (e.g., a Bitcoin mining rig, a secure communication hub, a cloud storage vault). A friendly, approachable robot character is shown easily installing new services from the stalls onto a central, glowing server core. Small, clear UI elements showing installation progress and service status are subtly integrated. The overall mood is one of empowerment and simplicity, showcasing how StartOS makes complex self-hosting accessible. 3D isometric illustration style with clean lines and a warm, inviting color palette.
